channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: More Americans Than Ever Are Taking Weight-Loss Drugs
item.description: The number of U.S. adults taking an obesity medication has nearly quadrupled from just two years ago.
item.dc:creator: Dani Blum

media:content.media:credit: Cydni Elledge for The New York Times

item.title: Word of the Day: deficit
item.description: This word has appeared in 972 articles on NYTimes.com in the past year. Can you use it in a sentence?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Deadly Typhoon Bursts Dam Wall as Heavy Rains Inundate China
item.description: Typhoon Maysak has been lashing Southern China for days, bursting dam walls and inundating homes and farms.
item.dc:creator: Jiawei Wang

media:content.media:description: A flooded riverside near a wharf on the Yongjiang river in Nanning, in China’s southern Guangxi Province, on Monday.

item.title: Las empresas de IA ahora contratan a filósofos
item.description: Los laboratorios de IA están contratando a sabios inconformistas, de esos que se frotan la barbilla y juntan los dedos en forma de pirámide. ¿Cuál desempleo?
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Wallace

media:content.media:credit: Aaron Wojack para The New York Times (top left); Ian C. Bates para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: En el sentido del reloj, desde la izquierda arriba: David Chalmers, Dillon PLunkett, Rosie Campbell y Robert Long

item.title: Wikipedia lucha por el alma de internet
item.description: La popular enciclopedia gratuita enfrenta ataques de la derecha, el avance de la inteligencia artificial y la presión de gobiernos que buscan controlar la información.
item.dc:creator: Tiffany Hsu

item.title: Dior and the Taylor Swift Wedding Effect
item.description: Were there clues to the dress in the brand’s couture show?
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Friedman

media:content.media:description: The fern-festooned wedding dress that closed the Dior couture show.

item.title: Soy psicólogo. No todo el mundo debería ir a terapia
item.description: La terapia parece haberse convertido en la receta por defecto para todo tipo de infelicidad.
item.dc:creator: Harvey Lieberman

item.title: Gotham F.C., the Women’s Soccer Team, Is Leaving New Jersey for Queens
item.description: After years of sharing a stadium with the Red Bulls in New Jersey, Gotham announces a partnership with the New York City Football Club at their new stadium in Willets Point, Queens.
item.dc:creator: David Waldstein
item.category: New York City Football Club (Soccer Team)

media:content.media:description: Carolyn Tisch Blodgett and Emily Tisch Sussman, owners of Gotham FC, show off the championship trophies of the club last season.

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Answers for July 7, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: How Economic Anxiety Is Driving the Democratic Socialists’ Surge
item.description: Interviews with D.S.A. members and organizers in New York City suggest that frustration with the economy has been the most important factor in the group’s growth.
item.dc:creator: Emma Goldberg

media:content.media:credit: Nicole Craine for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The Democratic Socialists of America’s New York City chapter has fostered a sense of community through running events and soccer games.

item.title: The New York City Plumber Who Made Over $465,000 in a Year
item.description: A public housing employee made more than $465,000 in a year with overtime, while apparently running two private plumbing businesses at the same time. The city is investigating.
item.dc:creator: Mihir Zaveri

media:content.media:description: Many of New York City’s aging public housing developments require constant maintenance. But one plumber working for the public housing agency logged an extraordinary amount of overtime.

item.title: Trump to Attend NATO Summit in Turkey
item.description: President Trump is expected to discuss American weaponry, NATO spending and the Russia-Ukraine war during a NATO summit in Ankara, Turkey.
item.dc:creator: Shawn Paik

media:content.media:description: NATO leaders at last year’s summit in The Hague, where member states committed to increase spending on defense sharply by 2035.

item.title: New Strikes on Ships in Strait of Hormuz Test U.S.-Iran Cease-Fire
item.description: A U.S. official said Iranian missiles struck two commercial ships. There was no immediate comment from Iranian officials on the reported attacks.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt and Qasim Nauman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Vessels anchored near Muscat, Oman, last month.

item.title: ‘Kimmel’ Guest Host Tiffany Haddish Roasts Trump’s Fourth of July Party
item.description: The comedian and actress joked that “there was more people at my bat mitzvah” than at America’s 250th birthday party.
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix

item.title: At Least 15 Dead in China After Severe Flooding During Typhoon Maysak
item.description: Videos showed inundated roads, farmland and buildings, and floodwaters rushing toward homes, while the region braced for a potential super typhoon this week.
item.dc:creator: Lily Kuo and Pei-Lin Wu

media:content.media:description: A flooded riverside near a wharf on the Yongjiang river in Nanning, in China’s southern Guangxi Province, on Monday.

item.title: ¿Qué significa hoy en día ‘arreglarse’?
item.description: Una lectora se pregunta si es bueno o malo que la gente vaya cada vez más informal.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Friedman

media:content.media:description: One designer’s definition of dressing up allows for “perfectly fitting jeans and a crisp white T-shirt” — provided there is emphasis on the “perfectly fitting” and the “crisp” parts.

item.title: JD Vance Is Worried
item.description: The vice president’s theology comes in book form.
item.dc:creator: Carlos Lozada
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
Communion: Finding My Way Back to Faith (Book)

media:content.media:credit: Paola Chapdelaine for The New York Times

item.title: Behind the Scenes of NATO, Europe Is Falling Apart
item.description: The greatest dangers lie within.
item.dc:creator: David Broder

item.title: Joining a Human Tide of Mourners Flowing Through Tehran’s Streets
item.description: After days of public mourning ceremonies in the Iranian capital, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei’s coffin arrived in Tehran’s Azadi Square. This is what I saw covering the miles-long procession on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Abdi Latif Dahir and Emile Ducke

media:content.media:description: Crowds gathered around the coffin of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei in Tehran, Iran, on Monday.

item.title: Quote of the Day: Ankara Moves to Stifle Critics Amid Summit
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Tuesday, July 7, 2026.

item.title: In NATO’s Next Act, Can Europe Play the Leading Role?
item.description: The focus at a leaders’ summit in Turkey this week will be on building a new model for the alliance, as President Trump pushes to do less and pressures others do more.
item.dc:creator: Steven Erlanger and Lara Jakes
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:description: NATO leaders at last year’s summit in The Hague, where member states committed to increase spending on defense sharply by 2035.

item.title: A Long-Awaited Verdict for Marine Le Pen Could Reshape France’s Politics
item.description: A court will decide on Tuesday whether to uphold Ms. Le Pen’s embezzlement conviction. If it does, the far-right leader will probably be barred from running for the presidency next year.
item.dc:creator: Mark Landler

media:content.media:description: Marine Le Pen met with supporters at a country fair in Liévin, France, on Saturday.

item.title: Fans Celebrate Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce’s Wedding With Charitable Donations
item.description: Inspired by Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce’s $26 million charitable gifts ahead of their wedding, Swifties are expressing their own generosity through donations to causes the couple supports.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Lyon

media:content.media:credit: Lanna Apisukh for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Fans of Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce are making their own charitable donations in honor of the newlyweds.

item.title: Trump’s Tenuous Relationship With NATO Allies
item.description: At this year’s NATO summit in Turkey, President Trump is arriving with his own priorities — and, as Tyler Pager, The Times’s White House correspondent, explains, they do not necessarily align with those of other NATO leaders.
item.dc:creator: Tyler Pager, Nour Idriss, Nikolay Nikolov, Stephanie Swart, Rafaela Balster and Ray Whitehouse

item.title: Samsung Made More Profit Last Quarter Than the Last Two Years Combined
item.description: The A.I. boom has propelled the South Korean tech giant’s earnings, but its shares sank as investors had even loftier expectations.
item.dc:creator: Daisuke Wakabayashi and Jason Karaian

item.title: Muere el ‘Indio’ Solari, leyenda del rock argentino
item.description: Un icono de la contracultura con gran atractivo, se comparaba a sí mismo con Bob Dylan y se ganó un público especialmente fiel entre la juventud de clase trabajadora.
item.dc:creator: Adam Nossiter

media:content.media:description: El “Indio” Solari en su último concierto público en 2017. Frases de sus canciones enigmáticas, llenas de personajes ficticios y metáforas esotéricas, terminaron apareciendo en tatuajes por toda Argentina.

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for July 7, 2026
item.description: Brad Lively gets sentimental.
item.dc:creator: Sam Corbin

media:content.media:description: How to solve a tricky crossword puzzle? Step by step, of course.

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for July 8, 2026
item.description: Mason Hyunjin Lee makes his New York Times Crossword debut.
item.dc:creator: Sam Corbin

media:content.media:description: Stumped by today’s crossword? Look at it this way: Things can only go up from here.

item.title: U.S. Loss Is a Gut Punch, but a Bright Future Remains for Soccer Here
item.description: While the team failed to meet the hopes of many fans, this World Cup highlighted the game’s decades-long climb into the national consciousness. What comes next?
item.dc:creator: David Waldstein

media:content.media:description: Folarin Balogun of the United States during the team’s 4-1 loss to Belgium in Seattle on Monday. The team was eliminated from the World Cup, but soccer stakeholders are upbeat about the sport’s future.

item.title: Lessons From the Graham Platner Disaster
item.description: Many Democrats want representatives as furious as they are. That makes it easier for unfit candidates to escape proper vetting.
item.dc:creator: Michelle Goldberg

media:content.media:credit: Sophie Park for The New York Times

item.title: In CNN Interview, Woman Details Alleged Sexual Assault by Graham Platner
item.description: “This is something that I tried for many years to forget,” the woman, Jenny Racicot, said on CNN.
item.dc:creator: Bayliss Wagner

media:content.media:credit: Sophie Park for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Graham Platner has faced calls from Democrats to drop out of the Maine Senate race after an allegation of sexual assault by Jenny Racicot, a woman he once dated.

item.title: Death Toll From Oppressive Heat in New Jersey May Be as High as 29
item.description: In New York City, emergency rooms last week logged the highest number of patients treated for heat-related illnesses in years.
item.dc:creator: Tracey Tully and Joseph Goldstein

media:content.media:description: Soccer fans sought a cool spot last week at a World Cup match at MetLife Stadium in New Jersey.

item.title: The World Cup Revived Seattle’s Downtown. Will the Energy Last?
item.description: The city struggled for years after the pandemic shutdown and the rush to remote work. But downtown has been full of soccer fans for the past month.
item.dc:creator: Tim Arango and Mike Baker

media:content.media:description: Seattle has used the World Cup as an opportunity to revitalize its downtown by drawing visitors to watch parties and celebrations before games. Local leaders have also tried to move chronically homeless people into shelters.

item.title: Intense Storms Cause Flash Flooding Across Northeast
item.description: Over the weekend, sporadic and dangerous storms hit Connecticut, New York, New Jersey and Pennsylvania with torrential rain and winds up to 90 miles per hour. The inclement weather continued on Monday, leading to flash flooding.
item.dc:creator: Meg Felling

media:content.media:credit: Angelina Katsanis for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Rain flooded streets in East Harlem in Manhattan last July when storms hit the region.

item.title: Trump Wanted a U.S. Soccer Star to Play in the World Cup. FIFA Found a Way.
item.description: There was nothing unusual about a call from President Trump or the reversal of Folarin Balogun’s suspension, said the president of FIFA, Gianni Infantino.
item.dc:creator: Tariq Panja

media:content.media:credit: Allison Robbert for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: FIFA’s president, Gianni Infantino, speaking to President Trump during an Oval Office visit in November.

item.title: Trump Expected to Tell Turkey He Is Ready to Restore Access to F-35 Jets
item.description: The president, who is headed to a NATO summit in Ankara this week, had imposed the ban himself amid concerns that giving Turkey the jets could allow Russia to learn about their stealth technology.
item.dc:creator: Tyler Pager and David E. Sanger
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:description: A model of an F-35 in the Oval Office. Turkey has been lobbying furiously for readmission to the fighter jet program for years.

item.title: Platner Faces Growing Democratic Calls to Drop Out
item.description: Even supporters like Representative Ro Khanna of California and Senator Elizabeth Warren of Massachusetts called on the Democratic nominee to exit the Maine Senate race after a report that he had sexually assaulted a woman.
item.dc:creator: Emily Davies and Shane Goldmacher

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Representative Ro Khanna, a California Democrat who had been one of Graham Platner’s most vocal supporters, said on Monday that he was withdrawing his endorsement.

item.title: Trump Tries to Take Credit for Walmart Price Cuts of Beef and Other Products
item.description: The retailer said it will lower prices for beef, produce, drinks, pools, toys, grills and summer clothing.
item.dc:creator: Kim Bhasin

media:content.media:description: A Walmart store in Chicago.

item.title: Who Might Replace Graham Platner if He Drops Out of the Maine Senate Race?
item.description: Graham Platner can be replaced on the ballot if he withdraws in the next week. If he does, Maine Democrats would face an uncertain two-week race to choose a replacement.
item.dc:creator: Reid J. Epstein

media:content.media:credit: Sophie Park for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Graham Platner won the Democratic nomination for Senate in early June, but he could still be replaced on the ballot.

item.title: Detective Struck by Friendly Fire in Brooklyn Shooting, Police Say
item.description: The police had first said the detective, who was wearing a bulletproof vest, was shot by a man in the street early Sunday. On Monday, they said that in fact another officer accidentally shot him.
item.dc:creator: Chelsia Rose Marcius

media:content.media:credit: Kyle Mazza/Sipa USA, via Associated Press
media:content.media:description: A detective was accidentally shot in the back when another officer fired a weapon while confronting an armed man, the police said.

item.title: Trump tiene un nuevo roce con Europa, ahora por el fútbol
item.description: La decisión de la FIFA de revocar la suspensión de un partido de un jugador de la selección de Estados Unidos cayó muy mal en Europa, en un momento en el que la tensión geopolítica ya era alta.
item.dc:creator: Jeanna Smialek and Ali Watkins

media:content.media:description: El delantero estadounidense Folarin Balogun, a la derecha, cuya tarjeta roja en un partido del Mundial la semana pasada le supuso una suspensión de un partido, ha recibido el visto bueno para jugar por parte del organismo rector del fútbol.

item.title: What Polls Say About the Fight for the Senate
item.description: Two of our political reporters dig into a revealing week of surveys.
item.dc:creator: Katie Glueck

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times

item.title: Inside Trump’s Ideological Fight With the Smithsonian
item.description: Publicly and behind the scenes, the president continues to try to impose his own views of American history and culture, presenting an ongoing challenge to Lonnie Bunch, the institution’s leader.
item.dc:creator: Maggie Haberman and Jonathan Swan
item.category: National Museum of African American History and Culture

media:content.media:credit: Lawren Simmons for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump and his allies have criticized what they call “improper ideology” in the Smithsonian’s presentation of American history and culture.

item.title: Trump Fuels a Furious World Cup Debate
item.description: Also, Platner says he will take time to “reflect” after a new accusation. Here’s the latest at the end of Monday.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Cullen

media:content.media:description: Folarin Balogun after scoring against Bosnia and Herzegovina last week.

item.title: Lemon-Garlic Grilled Chicken Breasts
item.description: This smoky beauty of a recipe, linked below, with its juicy swirl of lemon and olive oil, its fragrant charred garlic and parsley, is mouthwatering in the primal way of fried drumsticks and saucy wings.

item.title: Indio Solari, Argentine Rocker Who Packed Stadiums, Dies at 77
item.description: A counterculture icon with broad appeal, he compared himself to Bob Dylan and developed an especially fervent following among working-class youth.
item.dc:creator: Adam Nossiter

media:content.media:description: Indio Solari at his last public concert, in 2017. Phrases from his cryptic songs, filled with fictional characters and esoteric metaphors, found their way onto tattoos and T-shirts across Argentina.

item.title: Key Health Care Panel Is Again Blocked From Meeting Under RFK Jr.
item.description: The United States Preventive Services Task Force has been stymied under Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
item.dc:creator: Maggie Astor

media:content.media:description: Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. canceled meetings of the United States Preventive Services Task Force last July, last November and this March.

item.title: Timeline: How FIFA’s Reversal on Balogun Unfolded
item.description: Folarin Balogun’s red-card suspension was overturned by FIFA. President Trump said he asked for the review that ultimately led to the reversal.
item.dc:creator: Martín González Gómez, Tariq Panja and Tyler Pager

item.title: Judge Appointed Last Year Is Overseeing Hearing in Killing of Charlie Kirk
item.description: Judge Tony Graf previously served as a prosecutor in Utah, including in the county where Mr. Kirk was shot and killed last year.
item.dc:creator: Madaleine Rubin

media:content.media:description: Judge Tony Graf at court in April.

item.title: Calls Mount for Platner to End Campaign After Rape Accusation
item.description: After a woman accused Graham Platner, the Democratic Senate nominee in Maine, of rape, he denied the allegation but said he was taking time to “reflect” on his path forward.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: Sophie Park for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Graham Platner has faced calls from Democrats to drop out of the Maine Senate race after an allegation of sexual assault by Jenny Racicot, a woman he once dated.
